productionEpochPolicy.closeRule: An epoch closes only after all creation targets and its bounded refinement-and-pruning slice pass validation and publication.

legacyPlayableUpgrades.note: Legacy playable upgrades remain excluded from new-production creation counts.

lastCatalogUnit.result: Accepted 10 genuinely new natural-language kit contracts after six additional duplicate, catalog-overlap, or blurred-scope rejections; no implementation was created.

lastCreationUnit.result: Upgraded Next Ledge so Slipstream Launch and Cacheline High converge at Windglass Relay, banking 134 preserved speed or 175 cargo mastery before generic ascent resumes; production creation counts remain unchanged.

lastRefinementUnit.scope: Signal Bastion player-first command and terminal recovery refinement
lastRefinementUnit.result: The formerly unscored legacy route now opens with one mission owner, three starter towers, a physical wave command, readable combat pressure, terminal core breach, and a visible rebuild action while the readiness stack remains available only through Advanced diagnostics.

lastCompletedUnit.scope: Signal Bastion complete first-command defense loop
lastCompletedUnit.result: A player can read the purpose, place Bolt, Ember, and Frost towers, start and clear waves, observe one authoritative objective/impact path, lose the hard route, and rebuild the complete run. Three readiness surfaces and their 48 diagnostic descriptors are absent from the default player view and restored only by the explicit Advanced toggle.
lastCompletedUnit.validation: Twenty-two existing Signal Bastion tests passed after excluding the unchanged legacy-package executable smoke that still imports missing nexusrealtime dependencies; affected JavaScript syntax, JSON, and diff hygiene passed. The repository-wide runner was invoked and stopped only at the starting-commit duplicate drawHexFill declaration in experiments/The Cavalry of Rome/src/hex-gameplay-pass.js. Playwright physically completed three-tower placement, two wave commands, clear, hard-route breach, rebuild, and Advanced closed/open/closed loops with zero console errors. Human View passed first screen, line ready, active wave, terminal breach, and rebuild. Matched active performance improved from 82.946 to 33.315 ms average frame time, 12.056 to 30.02 measured FPS, and 87.5 to 35.3 ms p95; default DOM fell 138 to 108, canvases 4 to 1, descriptors 48 to 24, heap 33.452 to 30.283 MB, and long tasks held at zero in the final available signal.
lastCompletedUnit.publication: ProtoKits production commit bb3d787da372bf001653635d6e57eb7ce54e3c50 and Experiments production commit c737a9795fa2d94d18d2e16d3c11655ea5a0857e published to their resolved default branch main after fetch-before-push verification; this repository commit records the publication receipt.

exactNextUnit.goal: After the first clear, present one authored choice between upgrading the starter line and adding a specialist tower, reusing existing economy, session, wave-preview, and descriptor owners without another first-screen control or state owner.
